question: Will Gold (GC) hit (LOW) $4,000 by end of March?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or below the listed price by the final trading day of March 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket tilts toward gold futures (GC) surpassing key resistance levels by March 31, driven primarily by persistent expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts amid cooling inflation data. Spot gold trades near $2,670/oz, with COMEX futures implying a 55% probability of hitting $2,750 amid dollar weakness (DXY at 103.5) and rising real yields pressuring bullion lower short-term. Central bank purchases—over 1,000 tonnes in 2024 per World Gold Council—and geopolitical risks in Ukraine and Middle East bolster upside. Watch March 19 FOMC for cut signals and Feb 12 CPI release; a print below 2.9% YoY could propel prices toward $2,800, while hawkish Fed rhetoric risks a pullback to $2,600 support.

question: Panthers vs. Rangers
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for March 29 at 1:00PM ET:
If the Panthers win, the market will resolve to "Panthers".
If the Rangers win, the market will resolve to "Rangers".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Florida Panthers hold a slim edge in trader sentiment for their Eastern Conference Finals matchup against the New York Rangers, with markets implying about 55% win probability for Game 4 tonight amid Sam Bennett's return boosting Florida's top line after missing three games with a hand injury. Igor Shesterkin's .935 save percentage anchors New York's resilience despite a 2-1 series deficit, while Aleksander Barkov's two-way dominance and Sergei Bobrovsky's 2.78 GAA have propelled the Panthers' 7-1 road playoff record. Momentum favors Florida post their 3-2 overtime win in Game 3, but Rangers' home-ice power play (37.5%) and rest advantage could shift odds if Mika Zibanejad exploits matchups. Historical head-to-head splits even at 5-5 since 2021 underscore upset potential.

eventMetadata.context_description: Boston Bruins enter their matchup against the Columbus Blue Jackets as heavy trader favorites, reflecting their elite Atlantic Division standing and superior 8-2-1 road record this season. Key driver: Bruins' offensive firepower led by David Pastrnak's 12 goals, versus Columbus' league-worst 2.4 goals per game amid rebuilding woes. Recent developments include no major injuries on Boston's official report, while Blue Jackets' Elvis Merzlikins returns from minor maintenance with a 3.10 GAA. Head-to-head history favors Bruins 6-4 in last 10, but Columbus' home desperation in playoff chase could spark upset potential. Watch rest advantage for Boston after back-to-back wins.

question: Will there be at least 1500 measles cases in the U.S. by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there have been the specified amount or more confirmed cases of Measles (Rubeola) in humans in the territory of the United States of America in 2026, according to the CDC case counter by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter 'Total Cases' in 2026 (see: https://www.cdc.gov/measles/data-research/index.html) at the resolution time. If the counter becomes unavailable, another credible source will be used.

Note: Only cases reported by the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter will qualify, regardless of reports from U.S. State agencies or other sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for U.S. measles cases by March 31 hinges on a sharp early-2024 surge, with CDC data showing 102 confirmed cases across 16 jurisdictions as of March 28—more than double 2023's total and the highest start since 2019's nationwide outbreak. Most stem from Chicago's Pilsen shelter outbreak among unvaccinated migrants, spilling into schools, amplified by measles' R0 of 12-18 in under-vaccinated pockets below 95% MMR coverage. Historical baselines show cases often cluster post-importation; models suggest potential for 150+ if exposures persist. Key watch: CDC's April 5 weekly update finalizing March 31 tally, plus state health alerts on containment.

question: US x Iran ceasefire by May 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, an “official ceasefire agreement” requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the United States and Iran, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, a wide consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Escalating Israel-Iran exchanges and Iran's proxy attacks via Hezbollah and Houthis drive low trader consensus for a US-Iran ceasefire, as Washington prioritizes Israel's security amid stalled nuclear talks. Recent developments include Israel's October 26 strikes on Iranian missile sites following Tehran's limited barrage, with US forces aiding defenses but urging restraint to avoid wider war. Biden administration channels through Oman yielded no breakthroughs, per official readouts, while Tehran demands sanctions relief unmet by demands for verifiable nuclear curbs. Upcoming US election results could pivot policy—Trump favors pressure, Harris continuity—keeping odds depressed amid high uncertainty in diplomatic momentum.

question: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by May 15?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Israeli airstrikes on Iranian military targets on October 26, following Iran's October 1 missile barrage, have tempered escalation risks without provoking a major Iranian retaliation, driving trader consensus toward prolonged but contained Iran-Israel/US tensions. Iran's downplayed response and focus on proxies like Hezbollah—amid Israel's Lebanon ground offensive—suggests no immediate full-scale war, though sporadic exchanges persist. Diplomatic calls from the US, G7, and UN for restraint add de-escalation pressure, but unresolved Gaza/Hezbollah fronts and the US presidential election on November 5 could shift dynamics. Markets reflect uncertainty, with "wisdom of crowds" pricing extended timelines over swift resolution.

question: Netanyahu out by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Benjamin Netanyahu ceases to be Prime Minister of Israel for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Benjamin Netanyahu's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Benjamin Netanyahu and the government of Israel; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Netanyahu out by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Benjamin Netanyahu announces that he will resign as Prime Minister of Israel, or otherwise steps down from/is removed from this position by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Note that an announcement of Benjamin Netanyahu's resignation or removal before the resolution date will suffice to resolve this market to "Yes" regardless of if/when he actually steps down.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the State of Israel, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Netanyahu's tenure as Israeli prime minister reflects his coalition's narrow stability amid Gaza war backlash, Hezbollah escalations, and ongoing corruption trials, with no-confidence motions failing in recent Knesset sessions. Primary drivers include stalled hostage negotiations and public protests eroding support—polls show opposition leaders like Benny Gantz leading hypothetically—but snap elections remain unlikely before 2026 absent major fractures. The ICC's May arrest warrant adds international pressure without domestic ouster mechanism. Upcoming trial testimonies in December and potential Lebanon cease-fires could sway sentiment, as traders weigh his history of political survival against rising dissent.

question: Will David Njoku play for Arizona Cardinals in 2026-27?
description:
This market will resolve to the next team David Njoku officially joins by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If David Njoku does not officially join a new team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If David Njoku joins a team that is not listed, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If David Njoku is released, retires, or is not under contract with any professional team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

An official signing announcement prior to the market’s close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official announcements from the NFL and/or the acquiring team. A consensus of credible media reporting may also be used to resolve the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: David Njoku's three-year extension with the Cleveland Browns expires after the 2026 season, positioning the 2026-27 campaign as his unrestricted free agency year and fueling trader bets on a move away from the rebuilding Browns (just 4.5%). The razor-thin gap between New England Patriots (48.1%) and Chicago Bears (47.5%) reflects both teams' urgent needs for a reliable pass-catching tight end to support young quarterbacks Drake Maye and Caleb Williams, respectively, amid ample projected cap space. Miami (39.0%) and Denver (33.0%) hover close due to similar TE voids and offensive upgrades around Tua Tagovailoa and Bo Nix, with the market's tight clustering underscoring widespread demand for Njoku's 2023 breakout production (882 yards, 6 TDs) despite his early 2024 ankle injury recovery.

This market will resolve to the next team David Njoku officially joins by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If David Njoku does not officially join a new team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If David Njoku joins a team that is not listed, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If David Njoku is released, retires, or is not under contract with any professional team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

An official signing announcement prior to the market’s close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official announcements from the NFL and/or the acquiring team. A consensus of credible media reporting may also be used to resolve the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: David Njoku's three-year extension with the Cleveland Browns expires after the 2026 season, positioning the 2026-27 campaign as his unrestricted free agency year and fueling trader bets on a move away from the rebuilding Browns (just 4.5%). The razor-thin gap between New England Patriots (48.1%) and Chicago Bears (47.5%) reflects both teams' urgent needs for a reliable pass-catching tight end to support young quarterbacks Drake Maye and Caleb Williams, respectively, amid ample projected cap space. Miami (39.0%) and Denver (33.0%) hover close due to similar TE voids and offensive upgrades around Tua Tagovailoa and Bo Nix, with the market's tight clustering underscoring widespread demand for Njoku's 2023 breakout production (882 yards, 6 TDs) despite his early 2024 ankle injury recovery.

Miami (39.0%) and Denver (33.0%) hover close due to similar TE voids and offensive upgrades around Tua Tagovailoa and Bo Nix, with the market's tight clustering underscoring widespread demand for Njoku's 2023 breakout production (882 yards, 6 TDs) despite his early 2024 ankle injury recovery.

question: Will David Njoku play for Jacksonville Jaguars in 2026-27?
description:
This market will resolve to the next team David Njoku officially joins by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If David Njoku does not officially join a new team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

An official signing announcement prior to the market’s close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official announcements from the NFL and/or the acquiring team. A consensus of credible media reporting may also be used to resolve the market.

question: Will any E.U. country strike Iran by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any E.U. member state initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by the listed entity's military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by a missile launched by the listed country, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by ground operatives of the listed country will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Which countries will conduct military action against Iran by March 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed country initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by the listed country's military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by a missile launched by the listed country, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by ground operatives of the listed country will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probabilities for major countries launching direct military action against Iran by March 31, driven by sustained restraint despite heightened Middle East tensions. Israel conducted limited strikes on Iranian targets in October 2024 but has since focused on proxies like Hezbollah in Lebanon and Hamas in Gaza, with no new announcements of Iran-focused operations. The US continues Houthi strikes in Yemen— Iran-backed but not mainland Iran—while emphasizing diplomacy on Tehran's nuclear program amid IAEA reports of non-compliance. Incoming Trump administration signals tougher Iran policy post-January 20, but absent concrete triggers like proxy escalations, markets price stability; watch for UN Security Council sessions or Israeli security cabinet decisions.

eventMetadata.context_description: Joey Bosa's contract with the Los Angeles Chargers expires after the 2025 season, positioning him as a top-tier free agent edge rusher for 2026-27 and fueling evenly split trader odds across cap-rich contenders needing pass-rush firepower. Recent injury recoveries have boosted his appeal—logging key sacks in 2024 despite foot concerns—but persistent health questions cap his price tag, preventing any team from dominating sentiment. Teams like the Panthers, Titans, and Bears lead at 48-47.5% implied probabilities due to glaring defensive gaps, projected $70M+ cap space, and rebuild timelines aligning with Bosa's prime at age 31, while the Chargers lag at 34% amid their own rebuild and Khalil Mack duo. This broad viability keeps the market razor-tight, embodying crowd wisdom on uncertain FA dynamics.

question: Will Emmanuel Macron be the next leader out before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve according to the first individual who ceases to occupy their listed office.

An announcement of a resignation/removal, or a scheduled departure from office due to the outcome of an election, will not alone qualify.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify for resolution. Temporary removals, such as impeachment suspensions (e.g., Yoon Suk Yeol's recent impeachment), temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment, or any similar provisional transfers of power, will not count.

Additionally, if an individual continues in a caretaker or interim role (e.g., Gabriel Attal remaining as caretaker Prime Minister of France), they will not be considered to have ceased occupying the office for the purposes of this market.

If this criterion has not been met for any of the listed individuals by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “None before 2027”. No additional individuals will be added to this market after its creation.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel trails at 21.5%, reflecting chronic blackouts, food shortages, and protest crackdowns amid economic collapse. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's 10.1% odds stem from Gaza war backlash, hostage crises, and fragile far-right coalition strains. Newer leaders like UK PM Keir Starmer (4.8%) face minimal pressure post-election honeymoon, underscoring Hungary's acute political volatility as the dominant market driver.

question: Nothing Ever Happens: March
description:
This market will resolve to “Something” if any of the following conditions are met between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET:

- Iranian Regime Falls
- Fed Rate Cut
- Trump declares election interference national emergency
- Insurrection Act invoked
- SAVE Act signed into law
- James Talarico and John Cornyn candidates for the Texas Senate Election

 Otherwise, this market will resolve to “Nothing”.

This market may remain open until the results of both Texas parties' primaries are known definitively.

The full rules for this market can be found here: 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/NEH-March.pdf

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for the "Nothing Ever Happens: March" market hinges on a lull in high-impact political catalysts midway through the month, with "Something" at 51.5% reflecting slim trader consensus on potential disruptions amid relative stability in US legislative gridlock, subdued diplomatic tensions, and steady economic indicators. Competitive balance stems from historical base rates of late-month surprises—like unexpected court rulings or agency announcements—offset by primary sources showing no imminent congressional votes or executive actions. Odds could tip toward "Something" on breaking developments such as Federal Reserve signals or international summits, while continued uneventful polling and routine briefings would bolster "Nothing," underscoring the wisdom of crowds in pricing political uncertainty.

question: Congress passes Iran war powers resolution by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if both the U.S. House of Representatives and the U.S. Senate pass the same bill, measure, or resolution that seeks to limit U.S. armed forces military action in the recent US/Israel-Iran conflict by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

Legislation will qualify as seeking to limit U.S. armed forces military action in the recent US/Israel–Iran conflict if it explicitly seeks to restrict, terminate, or require congressional approval for U.S. armed forces’ hostilities, strikes, deployments, or other military operations against Iran or its proxy forces. Non-binding statements or measures that express disapproval, call for investigation, or otherwise relate to the US/Israel-Iran conflict without seeking to limit military action will not qualify.

A measure amended by either chamber will only qualify if the amended version is subsequently finally passed by both chambers in identical form.

The resolution sources will be official congressional voting records and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly expects Congress to reject or fail to pass an Iran war powers resolution by March 31, driven by the absence of any active legislation advancing through committees or floor votes in either chamber. Recent U.S. airstrikes against Iran-backed Houthis in Yemen prompted Yemen-specific war powers measures, like Sen. Kaine's resolution, but no equivalent Iran-focused bill has gained sponsors or momentum amid distractions from government funding deadlines and Ukraine-Israel aid debates. With the legislative calendar compressed and bipartisan divisions on Middle East authorizations persisting, procedural barriers reinforce high confidence in non-passage. A direct Iran escalation or surprise presidential request remains a slim wildcard for reversal.

question: Will the 2026 Men's NCAA basketball National Champion come from the ACC (Atlantic Coast Conference)?
description:
This market will resolve according to the Division I conference of the men’s basketball 2026 Division 1 NCAA Tournament champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for any team from the listed conference to win the 2026 NCAA Tournament championship game per the rules of the NCAA (e.g., team is eliminated), the corresponding market will resolve to "No".

If multiple teams are declared winners, this market will resolve in favor of the conference whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

If this event is cancelled, postponed after April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or a national champion has not been declared within this timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source will be official information from the National Collegiate Athletic Association (https://www.ncaa.com/sports/basketball-men/d1); however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Big Ten depth anchors its 39% trader consensus as top conference for NCAA Tournament national champion, with Purdue's Elite Eight triumph over Tennessee preserving the conference's premier contender alongside earlier advances by Illinois. Big 12's 27% hold stems from Houston's gritty win over Duke, showcasing defensive edge amid Iowa State's earlier exit, while SEC's 24% reflects Alabama's hot streak despite Kentucky and Tennessee flameouts. ACC odds at 18% surged on NC State and Duke's Cinderella surges before stumbles, buoyed by volume of bids. Recent Sweet 16 chaos, including upsets toppling seeds, underscores bracket volatility favoring conferences with surviving blue-bloods, as Mountain West's SDSU and Big East's UConn cling to underdog paths.

question: Cambria FDV above $20M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Cambria's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Cambria (https://x.com/playcambria) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Will Silver (SI) hit (HIGH) $200 by end of March?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Silver (SI) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of March 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Silver (SI) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (March, May, July, September, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month becomes a non-active month effective on its First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Silver (SI) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Silver futures (SI) trader sentiment on Polymarket tilts toward a "no" at 65% implied probability of staying below $32 by March 31, driven by persistent USD strength from hawkish Fed rhetoric and elevated real yields pressuring precious metals. Spot silver hovers near $31.20, up 28% YTD on industrial demand from solar panels and EVs, but faces headwinds from China's uneven stimulus and slowing global growth. Key catalysts ahead: February CPI release on March 12 and FOMC meeting March 18-19, where dot plot updates could signal fewer 2025 cuts, capping upside. Historical precedent shows silver struggling above $32 without sub-4% 10-year yields; traders watch $30.50 support for resolution risks.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Silver (SI) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of March 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Silver (SI) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (March, May, July, September, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month becomes a non-active month effective on its First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Silver (SI) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Silver futures (SI) trader sentiment on Polymarket tilts toward a "no" at 65% implied probability of staying below $32 by March 31, driven by persistent USD strength from hawkish Fed rhetoric and elevated real yields pressuring precious metals. Spot silver hovers near $31.20, up 28% YTD on industrial demand from solar panels and EVs, but faces headwinds from China's uneven stimulus and slowing global growth. Key catalysts ahead: February CPI release on March 12 and FOMC meeting March 18-19, where dot plot updates could signal fewer 2025 cuts, capping upside. Historical precedent shows silver struggling above $32 without sub-4% 10-year yields; traders watch $30.50 support for resolution risks.

question: Will AD Pasto win on 2026-03-29?
description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for March 29, 2026
If AD Pasto wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: AD Pasto vs. Atlético Nacional
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Colombia Primera A game, scheduled for Sunday, March 29, 2026 between AD Pasto and Atlético Nacional.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus slightly favors Atlético Nacional at 50% implied probability for their Liga BetPlay clash at AD Pasto's high-altitude Estadio Departamental Libertad, but Pasto's 49% and draw at 47.5% reflect intense parity driven by the hosts' unbeaten streak in five straight home matches and the venue's notorious oxygen-thin conditions that sap visiting legs. Nacional, mired in a three-game winless away skid despite superior head-to-head history (winning 4 of last 7), carries momentum risks from recent defensive lapses, while Pasto's pragmatic counterattacking style under coach Diego Corvalán exploits fatigue. No major injuries reported, leaving crowd wisdom split on whether Nacional's pedigree overcomes the environmental handicap.

question: Will Tommy Lloyd win the Men's 2026 Naismith Coach of the Year award?
description:
This market will resolve to the coach who wins the 2026 Naismith Coach of the Year award in Men’s Basketball.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by NCAA rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the coach whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 NCAA Men’s Basketball season is cancelled, postponed after April 30, 2026 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NCAA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: NCAAM: Naismith Coach of the Year
events.description:
This market will resolve to the coach who wins the 2026 Naismith Coach of the Year award in Men’s Basketball.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by NCAA rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the coach whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 NCAA Men’s Basketball season is cancelled, postponed after April 30, 2026 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NCAA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Naismith Coach of the Year award reflects a fiercely contested race, with Bill Self's 44.5% implied probability edging Josh Schertz's 43.0% amid debates over pedigree versus overachievement. Self steered Kansas through a grueling Big 12 schedule marred by injuries, securing a solid NCAA Tournament appearance, while Schertz orchestrated Indiana State's historic 32-7 campaign and Missouri Valley regular-season title, a stunning turnaround for the Sycamores. Dusty May's sustained FAU success (25-9, NCAA bid) and Fred Hoiberg's Nebraska revival (23-11) keep odds bunched above 37%, as semifinalists like Tom Izzo, Brad Underwood, and Mark Byington's James Madison (32-4) divide sentiment on metrics like win totals, efficiency, and roster rebuilds in the absence of a clear national champion.

question: Spread: Saudi Arabia (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 30 at 10:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Saudi Arabia" if Saudi Arabia win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Serbia".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on fifa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.
